angle_summary: Core Health and Performance has a strong online reputation with a perfect 5-star rating across 53 reviews, but the absence of an online booking system means motivated new clients cannot take action at the moment they are most ready to commit. A focused conversion improvement on their existing Wix site would close that gap without disrupting what is already working well.
proof_points: No online booking system is present, meaning prospective clients must submit a contact form or call to schedule, introducing friction at the most critical conversion point for a fitness and health business.

Mobile PageSpeed score of 63 indicates moderate performance on the devices most commonly used for local health and fitness searches, creating a secondary friction point at the moment of first site visit.
A 5-star Google rating across 53 reviews is not currently surfaced prominently above the fold, meaning first-time visitors may not encounter this trust signal before deciding whether to enquire.
offer_scope_points: Integrate a lightweight online booking solution using Wix Bookings or a compatible third-party scheduler, configured for the business's session or consultation types.
Update the hero section to prominently display the 5-star Google rating and review count as a visible trust badge, improving first-impression credibility for new visitors.
Audit and resolve mobile conversion friction points including click-to-call on the phone number, tap target sizing on CTAs, and image optimisation at mobile breakpoints to address the PageSpeed gap.
personalisation_notes: Core Health and Performance holds a perfect 5.0 rating across 53 Google reviews, which is an unusually strong trust asset for a local fitness business in Westport and should be referenced specifically in outreach to show the message is informed rather than generic.

The site is built on Wix, so all outreach should frame improvements as additions to the existing setup rather than a rebuild, directly addressing the likely objection that Wix limits what can be changed.
suggested_cta: Would you be open to a short walkthrough call where I can show you exactly how these changes would look on your current site?
avoid_saying: Avoid implying that manual bookings or the contact form are inadequate or unprofessional, as the business likely views its current enquiry process as functional and may be proud of how it operates.
Avoid suggesting the site needs to be rebuilt or that Wix is a limiting platform, as this is likely to trigger defensiveness and contradicts the recommended framing of improving what already exists.
unknowns_to_confirm: Whether the business owner has previously explored booking tools such as Wix Bookings and, if so, what prevented implementation, as this would clarify how to frame the booking system recommendation.
Whether there are specific service types, class formats, or session categories that would each require a separate booking flow, as this affects the scope and complexity of the conversion sprint.
